tools.name: get_knowledge_version
tools.description: Returns the MCP knowledge version: gitSha, indexedAt, componentCount, patternCount, uptimeSeconds. Call this ONCE per session before generating UI code so you know how fresh the design-system data is. Cheap to call. If gitSha is "unknown" or indexedAt is far in the past, surface that to the user bef

tools.name: get_design_guidelines
tools.description: MANDATORY FIRST CALL before writing any @marmoui/ui code in this session. Returns a step-by-step generation checklist (which tools to call, in what order), critical rules (no namespace sub-components, PageSection is self-closing, no Sidebar export), component patterns, and ICON LIBRARY RULES. Pass i

tools.name: list_components
tools.description: List all available @marmoui/ui components with names, descriptions, categories, and prop counts. Use to discover what exists. Prefer `search_components` when you already know the use case (form, table, dialog, etc.).

tools.name: search_components
tools.description: Search @marmoui/ui by keyword (e.g. "form", "table", "dialog", "avatar"). Preferred discovery tool — returns the 5-8 most relevant components for a need. Call this before `get_component_info` when unsure which component to use.

tools.name: get_component_info
tools.description: Get exact props (with types and defaults), code examples, composition patterns, and common mistakes for a single @marmoui/ui component. REQUIRED before using any component for the first time in a session — prevents hallucinated props (e.g. variant="danger" on Badge, size="lg" on Text) that fail at b

tools.name: get_pattern
tools.description: Get a complete, validated composition example for a common UI pattern. Available: app-layouts (ASK USER which of 4 layout ids before any full page), app-shell, confirmation-dialog, dialogs-for-other-actions, data-table-with-filters, form-with-validation, posthog-tracking. Call app-layouts first for

tools.name: review_generated_code
tools.description: ⚠️  MANDATORY — call this on every piece of code you generate before returning it to the user. Validates TSX/JSX against real @marmoui/ui prop signatures and returns { valid, errors[], warnings[], suggestedFixes[], iconLibrary }. Catches: (1) unknown imports, (2) Tabs.List/Tabs.Trigger namespace mis

tools.name: validate_component_usage
tools.description: Validate a TSX/JSX snippet against real @marmoui/ui prop signatures. Returns `{valid, issues[]}`. Prefer `review_generated_code` for post-generation review (it also auto-fixes namespace patterns and suggests related patterns). Use this tool when validating user-provided or existing code rather than

tools.name: get_tenant_theme
tools.description: Returns the authenticated tenant's customized theme tokens, CSS variables export, and component overrides. Call after auth to apply tenant-specific styling in generated code.

tools.name: get_design_md
tools.description: Generate a portable DESIGN.md for the authenticated tenant — brand color, type, tokens, and component inventory — that any AI agent can read to generate on-brand UI. Pass format="markdown" (default) for the DESIGN.md string or format="json" for structured data. Output is deterministic for a given te

tools.name: generate_component
tools.description: Validate and optionally save a custom component for the authenticated tenant. Runs review_generated_code validation before persisting. Use for AI-generated or customized components.

tools.name: get_agent_connect_config
tools.description: Returns ready-to-paste MCP configuration for Cursor, Claude Code, or Codex. Use on the Connect page or when setting up a new project.

tools.name: deploy_prototype
tools.description: Register a prototype for sharing and get a share URL. Reviewers can leave inline comments via the Marmo overlay.

tools.name: get_prototype_feedback
tools.description: Fetch inline comments from a shared prototype as structured data and Markdown. Pull into Cursor/Claude to iterate on feedback.

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "get_component_info" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Get exact props (with types and defaults), code examples, composition patterns, and common mistakes for a single @marmoui/ui component. REQUIRED before using an…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Read-only tool requests dangerous parameters
findings.detail: Tool "review_generated_code" appears informational but accepts parameters (code, context) that enable code/file/secret access.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: review_generated_code, deploy_prototype, get_prototype_feedback · Private data: get_tenant_theme, get_design_md · Public sink: validate_component_usage, deploy_prototype · Destructive: get_design_md.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: review_generated_code, deploy_prototype, get_prototype_feedback · Private data: get_tenant_theme, get_design_md · Public sink: validate_component_usage, deploy_prototype · Destructive: get_design_md

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
